mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for at least 1 month
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following trigger activation are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives retail sales and corporate earnings expectations upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions propagate through equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150,000 persons month-over-month for two consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with accelerating labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,000 thousand units signals weakening demand expectations and reduced future consumer wealth, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 105.0 index points combined with low unemployment (UNRATE below 4.2%) signals supply-side capacity constraints and inflationary pressure, which triggers equity market repricing downward as real discount rates rise, producing negative 20-day returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with supply constraints and wage pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO × UNRATE) shows Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.4%) signals monetary policy lag and future rate hikes, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as the market reprices terminal rate expectations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4% for two consecutive months
regime_hint: inflationary pressure with monetary tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the lagged correlation between CPIAUCSL growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand and earnings growth, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon as equity risk premiums compress and growth expectations are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, which depresses corporate earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as forward guidance is cut.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-stage recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 104.0 index points signal supply-side weakness and reduced economic capacity utilization, which depresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as growth forecasts are revised downward and recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 104.0 and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the lagged correlation between INDPRO levels and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
